title: tithymalopsis arundelana
intro: Tithymalopsis arundelana, also known as the Arundel tithymalopsis, is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae). It is native to the tropical regions of Central America and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, fighting infection, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Tithymalopsis arundelana can be made by drying the leaves and roots, then infusing them in hot water to create a tea, or by extracting the plant's resin to create a topical ointment.
